acceptance.assertion: Before implementation, the conventional Bureau checkout is reread. The four target dirty paths still match dirty diff SHA-256 e2d03bdd78032e65ebe6ca3f7a395e50af4699b39ec5a4b63723b2fc45fd820a from source snapshot c0eb44e90706709ef617f48e5b0f128608c5089b5c5681d7726db8512c7c1b88, or execution stops fail-closed. The conventional dirty checkout is never reset, cleaned, staged, committed, overwritten or force-released.

Current clean canonical Registry, Bureau lifecycle/Doctor, queue, GitHub PR bindings, live-register activity and Grabowski leases are reread immediately before effects. Only initiatives still reported completion-ready and represented by the preserved four-path scope may change; unrelated KONVERGENZREGELKREIS-V1 and all other dirty state remain untouched.

Still-valid closeouts are reproduced from current main in a new isolated leased worktree and change only state to completed, commitment to completed and canonical lifecycle.completed_at metadata as appropriate. No bytes are copied by overwriting the conventional dirty checkout.

bureau check, strict Registry truth, lifecycle/Doctor checks, git diff --check and the relevant full Bureau test suite pass on the exact final head. A complete head/diff-SHA-256-bound self-review reports PASS before PR publication or merge.

The final PR is bound to exactly this task, has green required CI and unchanged reviewed head before merge. Post-merge readback confirms origin/main contains only the reviewed closeouts, the canonical runtime snapshot is refreshed through the standard runtime-refresh contract if needed, and the preserved original dirty bytes are not silently discarded.

Any target skipped because lifecycle truth changed, ownership remains ambiguous, a foreign lease/PR overlaps, or preimage bytes differ is left untouched and registered as explicit follow-up rather than being forced through this task.

approval.note: The user ordered continued autonomous Bureau task completion. Implementation must preserve the conventional dirty checkout byte-for-byte, use an isolated leased worktree, and rederive lifecycle truth immediately before effects.

goal: Resolve the four remaining Bureau lifecycle mismatches without altering the pre-existing dirty conventional checkout: preserve and hash-bind its bytes, rederive current lifecycle truth, reproduce only still-valid semantic closeouts in an isolated leased worktree, validate and review the exact diff, then publish through normal PR/CI/merge/readback.

rollback.strategy: Revert only the merged T017 closeout commit or PR. Preserve the original conventional dirty bytes and historical candidate/audit snapshots; do not rewrite task, candidate, receipt or dirty-state history.

title: Publish four preserved dirty lifecycle closeouts from isolated truth
